notes.anchor: M. CICERO T. TITIO T. F. LEG. S. D.
notes.note: The letter-heading. T. f. = Titi filius ("son of Titus"); LEG. = legatus, a deputy or staff officer, here apparently attached to the grain administration. S. D. = salutem dicit, "sends greeting."

notes.anchor: both in the place to which he is to convey the grain and in the matter of the timing
notes.note: C. Avianius Flaccus was a grain-contractor (negotiator in the corn trade); the two concessions Cicero seeks for him are the delivery-point and the schedule for handing over the grain, both points of leverage in fulfilling a state grain contract.

notes.anchor: for three years while Pompey was in charge of that office
notes.note: Pompey held the cura annonae, an extraordinary five-year commission over the grain supply granted in 57 BC; while he ran it Avianius had been granted the same favourable terms, which Cicero now asks Titius to renew.
